perturbation.original_text: Now, Therefore , in consideration of the discussions and the sharing of information between the Parties, and the premises, conditions, covenants and warranties herein contained, the Parties agree as follows: 1. Definitions . For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean commercial, corporate, scientific, regulatory and technical information, including, without limitation, data, materials, documents, agreements, lists, financial information, investments, information concerning current or proposed products, technologies, applications, service or methods of operation and/or business plans, customers, suppliers, and any copies or versions of the information disclosed, relating to NEW CONFIDENTIAL RIVER or SHIRE, that NEW RIVER or SHIRE, respectively, regards as its highly valuable trade secrets, confidential information, and proprietary property.
perturbation.changed_text: Now, Therefore , in consideration of the discussions and the sharing of information between the Parties, and the premises, conditions, covenants and warranties herein contained, the Parties agree as follows: 1. Definitions . For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean commercial, corporate, scientific, regulatory and technical information, including, without limitation, data, materials, documents, agreements, lists, financial information, investments, information concerning current or proposed products, technologies, applications, service or methods of operation and/or business plans, customers, suppliers, and any copies or versions of the information disclosed, relating to NEW CONFIDENTIAL RIVER or SHIRE, that NEW RIVER or SHIRE, respectively, may consider as potentially sensitive, private or secret.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'regards as its highly valuable trade secrets, confidential information, and proprietary property' has been changed to 'may consider as potentially sensitive, private or secret'. This weakens the definition of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because later in the agreement (section 3, Non-Disclosure Obligation), the receiving party is obligated to protect the 'other party's Confidential Information in strict confidence.' If 'Confidential Information' is now ambiguously defined, the obligation to protect it becomes unclear, potentially leading to disputes over what information is actually protected under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence in accordance with this Agreement; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information (but in no event less than reasonable care); (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, independent contractors and consultants) without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party except as expressly permitted under Paragraph 4; and (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as something the party 'regards as its highly valuable trade secrets, confidential information, and proprietary property,' which implies a strong level of protection and value. The changed text weakens this definition to something the party 'may consider as potentially sensitive, private or secret.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which obligates each party to 'hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence.' The weakened definition in the changed text now introduces ambiguity about what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' making the obligation to protect it in strict confidence unclear and potentially unenforceable, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (b) “Disclosing Party” shall mean the Party disclosing Confidential Information. (c) “Receiving Party” Shall mean the Party receiving disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) “Releasing Entity” shall mean the Party disclosing Confidential Information. (c) “Receiving Party” Shall mean the Party receiving disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Disclosing Party' with 'Releasing Entity' introduces ambiguity. While the meaning is still relatively clear in this definition, the change creates a contradiction when referring back to the original term within the agreement. The agreement uses 'Disclosing Party' frequently, and failing to consistently use 'Releasing Entity' will create confusion on whether 'Disclosing Party' and 'Releasing Entity' are same, generating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall apply to all Confidential Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, and Confidential Information shall include extracts, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or records prepared by or for the Receiving Party or any of Receiving Party’s Representatives (defined in Paragraph 4) to the extent that such extracts, analyses, compilations, studies, documents or records contain or otherwise reflect or are generated from the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Disclosing Party' to 'Releasing Entity' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Disclosing Party'. The explanation explicitly states that this misalignment of terminology causes confusion and an in-text contradiction because the agreement uses 'Disclosing Party' frequently, and failing to consistently use 'Releasing Entity' will create confusion on whether 'Disclosing Party' and 'Releasing Entity' are same, generating an In-Text Contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Disclosure to Representatives, Conduct of Activities, and Prohibited Activities. (a) Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Disclosure to Representatives, Conduct of Activities, and Prohibited Activities. (a) Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Sensitive Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in Section 4 introduces a contradiction. Section 1(a) defines 'Confidential Information.' This change creates uncertainty in enforcement of who can disclose confidential information to whom, or what can be disclosed. This creates an In-Text contradiction and could generate disputes on which information is supposed to be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Sensitive Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each Party agrees to instruct all such Representatives (i) of the identities of the Party’s other permitted Representatives, and (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, subsidiaries, independent contractors, and consultants) other than the identified Representatives without the prior written permission of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in Section 4(a). The contradicted text then refers to the disclosure of 'Sensitive Information' while instructing representatives not to disclose 'Confidential Information.' Because Section 1(a) defines only 'Confidential Information', this change creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty, leading to potential disputes and impacting enforceability as it's unclear which type of information is subject to disclosure restrictions.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Non-Confidential Information . The confidentiality and non-use provisions of this Agreement shall not include information that the Receiving Party is able to demonstrate by competent proof: (a) is now or hereafter becomes, through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party, generally known or available within the industry without restriction as to its use or disclosure, or is now or later enters the public domain through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party; (b) was acquired or known by the Receiving Party before receiving such information from the Disclosing Party as shown by the Receiving Party’s written records; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party having a right to disclose it, without restriction as to use or disclosure;
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Unprotected Information . The confidentiality and non-use provisions of this Agreement shall not include information that the Receiving Party is able to demonstrate by competent proof: (a) is now or hereafter becomes, through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party, generally known or available within the industry without restriction as to its use or disclosure, or is now or later enters the public domain through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party; (b) was acquired or known by the Receiving Party before receiving such information from the Disclosing Party as shown by the Receiving Party’s written records; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party having a right to disclose it, without restriction as to use or disclosure;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the title from 'Non-Confidential Information' to 'Unprotected Information' creates inconsistency. The term 'Non-Confidential Information' is specifically defined in Section 5. Referencing this term in further sections, for example, Section 7 will cause ambiguity on whether it is the same, creating an In-Text contradiction. It can lead to an argument about which type of information the receiving party has to return upon request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will promptly return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof; provided, however, Receiving Party’s legal department or outside legal counsel may maintain a single copy of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for purposes of regulatory compliance and compliance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Non-Confidential Information' to 'Unprotected Information' creates a direct contradiction. The original text defined 'Non-Confidential Information' in Section 5, and the contradicted text references 'Confidential Information'. By changing the title in Section 5, it introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Unprotected Information' and the original 'Non-Confidential Information' are the same. This affects the interpretation of what materials the Receiving Party must return upon request as defined in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Based on the Perturbation Explanation and the definition of Misaligned Terminology, this change will lead to arguments about which type of information is being referenced in the contradicted text in section 7, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Removal of Confidential Materials . Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made without the consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Removal of Restricted Materials . Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made without the consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Materials' with 'Restricted Materials' in Section 6 introduces ambiguity. The lack of definition of what constitutes Restricted Materials creates uncertainty around what the receiving party is not permitted to remove. This will contradict Section 1(a) because Restricted Materials has not been defined, creating an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made without the consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Materials' with 'Restricted Materials' in Section 6. According to the Perturbation Explanation, the term 'Restricted Materials' is not defined, creating uncertainty and ambiguity. The Contradicted Text refers to confidential information, but now section 6 refers to Restricted Materials which could encompass a wider or entirely different set of materials. This misaligned terminology, as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, creates an in-text contradiction because the contract now uses 'Restricted Materials' without defining it, leading to conflicting obligations about what materials are covered.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Equitable and Legal Relief . Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is owned solely by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to seek an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Equitable and Legal Relief . Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Sensitive Data is owned solely by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to seek an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data' in the beginning of Section 11 introduces ambiguity. This creates a contradiction within the section because it fails to clearly define if it means the same thing as 'Confidential Information,' which is defined earlier in the document. It is referencing the same concept, but using different wording creates confusion and inconsistency, leading to an In-Text contradiction on what the Disclosing Party owns.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Sensitive Data is owned solely by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to seek an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data' in the beginning of Section 11. The original text defined ownership of 'Confidential Information', while the changed text introduces 'Sensitive Data' without clarifying if they are the same. Because the changed text fails to adequately define if 'Sensitive Data' means the same thing as 'Confidential Information,' it creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which references both terms and leads to uncertainty about what information the Disclosing Party owns.
